description:
# Why

Sub-question of bl-82a4 (symlink-based config symmetry). That ball makes `.balls/config.json` a regular file in standalone mode and a symlink to the state-repo's view in federated mode. The plugins map travels via that symlink, but per-plugin config files live in `.balls/plugins/<name>.json` — a separate directory, so the canonical-config symlink alone doesn't carry them. We need the per-plugin files to follow the same master-wins rule (bl-a7d9), which means the directory needs the same symlink treatment.

Splitting this out of bl-82a4 because the resolution is largely independent of the rest of that ball's design (pointer location, migration, etc.) and the user wants it captured as its own concern.

# Open design question

Two viable shapes:

(a) **Two parallel symlinks**:
  - `.balls/config.json` → `.balls/state-repo/.balls/config.json`
  - `.balls/plugins/` → `.balls/state-repo/.balls/plugins/`
  Two things to set up at remaster time; two things to flip at detach time.

(b) **Umbrella symlink** at a new path:
  - `.balls/canonical/` → `.balls/state-repo/.balls/` in federated mode
  - `.balls/canonical/` is a real directory in standalone mode
  One symlink to maintain. But callers everywhere have to learn the new path, and existing code paths reading `.balls/config.json` and `.balls/plugins/*` have to be retargeted.

Other knobs that bear on the choice:
- Atomicity of \"flip\" — replacing a directory with a symlink isn't a single syscall on most filesystems; need an order-of-operations design.
- Discoverability — option (b) hides the canonical layout behind one path; option (a) keeps current paths working.

# Concrete deliverable

- Pick one of (a)/(b) after design discussion.
- Implement so bl callers that currently read `.balls/plugins/<name>.json` continue to work (or get a one-shot migration).
- Tests covering: standalone (real dir), federated (symlink resolves), detach (symlink reverts to real dir).

# Out of scope

- The pointer-file vs canonical-file design at the top level — that's bl-82a4.
- bl plugin enable/disable tooling — that's bl-32e5; it composes orthogonally with whichever layout this ball picks.

# Origin

bl-a7d9 close-out (2026-05-20). User on the discussion-items list: \"3: file it.\" Referring to the granularity question (one symlink vs umbrella) for .balls/plugins/.
title: .balls/plugins/ symlink granularity under config symmetry
